description_long: This object is a compact solar energy infrastructure unit featuring a rectangular array of photovoltaic panels mounted on a robust frame. The top surface is covered with dark, grid-patterned solar cells, likely monocrystalline or polycrystalline silicon, encased in a metallic or composite frame. The side view reveals a series of support struts and a base structure, possibly housing electrical components such as junction boxes or inverters. The base appears to be reinforced, with visible yellow and black elements suggesting protective or mounting features. The overall design is modular, suitable for installation on flat surfaces such as rooftops, parking lots, or ground-level platforms. The structure is engineered for durability and efficient solar energy capture in urban environments.
description: A rectangular solar panel array with a sturdy frame and visible support structure, designed for efficient energy capture and urban installation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the narrow edge of the solar panel array and the reinforced base with yellow and black detailing.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the solar panels, support struts, and the extended base structure.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the base and panel frame visible but less detail on the support structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the length of the solar panels and the structural supports, mirroring view 1.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 20 degrees, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with an aluminum frame and steel support struts
